Session session-3e698e14-13ff-4ce4-b6bb-71e66734c6e4

How to read this report

  • A missing public report is missing evidence, never a zero-strength signal, unless a band-qualified activity census proves that reporter was active for that cycle.
  • This report describes evidence; it does not select a winner or prove one antenna is better.
  • Each comparison group (direction × band × mode × kind × source) is analyzed separately and never combined.
  • A block is a back-to-back pair of cycles, one per antenna.
  • Alternating antennas reduces but does not eliminate time and propagation effects.

Answer first

What did the run show?

Answered by this run: Shared-path signal; Observed reach; Observed distance and direction profile; Repeatability across blocks.

For this General coverage run in TX path · 20 m · WSPR · Imported spot · WSPR.live (delayed/public), DX Commander produced stronger recorded results: a +5 dB median across 83 shared paths, 133 versus 120 unique observed paths. These results describe this session, not a universal antenna ranking.

Shared-path signal
+5 dB median
83 shared paths · DX Commander stronger at the median
Observed footprint
133 vs 120 paths
DX Commander / Attic EFHW · uncontrolled observations
Station N1RWJ at FN41fr; goal: General coverage.

General coverage lens: Prioritizes shared-path signal, common listening opportunities, observed reach, distance and bearing, and repeatability. No universal antenna winner is selected.

The recorded goal changes presentation priority only; evidence, calculations, thresholds, and conclusions are unchanged.

Antennas
Attic EFHW / DX Commander
Bands
20 m
Direction / mode
TX path; Transmit-focused
Session state
Not recorded

Signed values: Positive values mean DX Commander was stronger; negative values mean Attic EFHW was stronger.

For scale: a 3 dB difference is the same change as doubling transmit power. Individual WSPR reports are whole-dB values that vary cycle to cycle.

Descriptive result by comparison group
Comparison group Path delta Paths / matched pairs Blocks (back-to-back cycle pairs, one cycle per antenna) Coverage
TX path · 20 m · WSPR · Imported spot · WSPR.live (delayed/public) -18 to +25 dB; median across paths +5 dB 83 / 327 7 Available

Not established by this run

Shared-path signal

Evidence basis: finite-SNR reports for the same remote path in both cycles of an eligible block, kept within each separate comparison group.

Signed values: Positive values mean DX Commander was stronger; negative values mean Attic EFHW was stronger. The vertical reference is zero.

Each dot is one unique remote path’s median across its matched pairs. Hover or focus a dot for its path, median delta, and matched-pair support. Axis markers show the signed dB scale; a 0 dB dot is retained as a true zero.

TX path · 20 m · WSPR · Imported spot · WSPR.live (delayed/public)

83 matched paths · 327 matched pairs · 7 blocks

Observed reach

Evidence basis: unique observed finite-SNR remote paths by antenna within each separate comparison group.

Counts are unique remote paths with usable signal reports within each comparison group. “Heard by both” supplies the path universe for same-path analysis; Attic EFHW-only and DX Commander-only paths remain visible.

TX path · 20 m · WSPR · Imported spot · WSPR.live (delayed/public)

Observed distance and direction profile

Evidence basis: all usable paths observed for each antenna in eligible blocks, including paths observed on only one antenna. Receiver/transmitter availability may have changed between antenna periods; this describes collected paths and is not a controlled detection comparison. This is not a radiation pattern, propagation model, or causal conclusion about observed or unobserved distances and directions, and it does not rank antennas universally.

Each remote path contributes once per antenna and exact comparison group. Observation support remains visible, but repeated observations from one endpoint do not inflate the footprint count. Near / local distance is a practical proxy only; propagation mode was not measured.

This separate view answers where finite-SNR differences occurred among paths decoded on both antennas.

Each located paired path contributes once to one fixed distance bin and one fixed 45° compass sector. The supporting paired-row count stays visible; repeated rows from one endpoint do not increase a cell’s path count.

Distance and azimuth path context

Distance and azimuth describe only the remote paths observed in these matched pairs. Missing location stays visible, and geographic concentration limits how broadly these paths represent other distances or directions.

Review derived solar context

Derived solar context

Solar elevation and light state are deterministic geometric context derived from UTC timestamps and explicit Maidenhead locator cell centers. They are not captured propagation observations, do not adjust comparison values, and do not establish a cause for an observed difference.

Algorithm: noaa-gml-fractional-year v1; coordinates: maidenhead-cell-center-v1. Daylight begins at 0°, civil twilight at −6°, nautical twilight at −12°, astronomical twilight at −18°; gray line denotes any twilight category.

Derived station and remote-endpoint solar context
Stratum Remote path Block Antenna Observation UTC time Endpoint Grid Coordinates Elevation Light state Gray line

Coverage overlap and repeatability

Observed complementarity counts unique paths that appeared in the recorded evidence. Opportunity-conditioned complementarity separately counts one-sided detections among receivers proven active during both cycles. Neither measures signal strength or becomes a composite antenna score.

TX path · 20 m · WSPR · Imported spot · WSPR.live (delayed/public)

Observed complementarity

Using both antennas produced 146 unique observed paths: 13 appeared only on Attic EFHW, 107 appeared on both, and 26 appeared only on DX Commander. The incremental recorded contributions were 13 beyond DX Commander and 26 beyond Attic EFHW.

Opportunity-conditioned complementarity unavailable: no common-active receiver census covers this comparison group. Observed-only paths do not substitute for that denominator.

Unique observed paths do not prove the other antenna could never reach those endpoints. Repeated block support is descriptive, not an inferential uncertainty statement, reliability probability, or recommendation to retain or remove an antenna.
